id: "18.1.17"

---

# UC-18.1.17 · Cisco ACI Multi-Site Health — NDO Schema Sync, ISN BGP-EVPN ASBR, Intersite L3Out, and Cloud APIC Stretch Visibility

## Description

Cisco **Multi-Site Orchestrator** lineage—today branded **Nexus Dashboard Orchestrator** (**NDO**) atop a **Nexus Dashboard** cluster—is the cross-fabric policy brain that publishes one intent catalog across physically separate Application Centric Infrastructure sites. Unlike intra-cluster APIC replication inside a single fabric, multi-site orchestration couples inventory rows for site identifiers, schema containers, templates, contracts, bridge domains, and VRF stitching so administrators deploy once while stretched overlays rely on Inter-Site Network (**ISN**) reachability plus spine-layer **BGP-EVPN ASBR** speakers exchanging routes toward remote-site peers.

When orchestrator snapshots drift toward **Out-of-Sync** states—often asymmetric per site—or audit feeds record stalled outcomes while remote APIC clusters pause, operators inherit split-risk posture where campus-specific forwarding continues yet declarative intent diverges. Cloud Network Controller footprints add Cloud APIC controllers and CSR1000v routers acting like cloud-side spokes so Splunk must correlate SaaS-plane telemetry with on-premises fabrics rather than assuming every symptom traces to one APIC quorum.

## Value

Treat this UC as an operational contract on multi-site fidelity: Splunk surfaces schema-deployment latency (**time_to_sync_seconds**) versus golden ceilings, BGP adjacency posture toward **Multi-Site Loopback** endpoints across the IPN/ISN underlay, Cloud APIC acknowledgement cadence, and APIC syslog faults referencing **multiSite::NotPresent**, giving planners evidence for convergence budgeting before stretched workloads flap.

Outcome themes include earlier detection of schema backlog versus baseline velocity so WAN planners react when ISN RTT climbs above sensible envelopes—often cited near fifty milliseconds active-active guidance—predictive cues when seconds-to-deploy climbs across waves meaning APIC saturation or overlay jitter, cleaner segregation between orchestrator outages that pause queues versus genuine fabric faults, and audit-ready linkage tying deployment phases back to templates without collapsing this lens into pure tenant CRUD workflows.

## Implementation

Deploy **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (**4022**), extend with **NDO** **`REST`** polls into **`cisco:mso:*`**/**`cisco:ndo:*`** sourcetypes, ingest spine **BGP-EVPN ASBR** telemetry as **`bgp:evpn:asbr`**, land **Cloud APIC** audit/health feeds, then schedule **`aci_uc1817_ndo_multisite_health`** with **`site_id`** throttles.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• **Multi-Site Orchestrator** (**NDO**/MSO) release aligned with Cisco guidance—plan NDO 4.x service bundles when Cloud Network Controller integrations matter—and APIC trains typical for stretched workloads (ACI 5.x/6.x families). Dedicated automation identities on **NDO** plus APIC clusters with outbound 443/tcp from Splunk collectors; rotate tokens used for REST pulls.
• Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (Splunkbase 4022) wherever **cisco:aci:syslog** lands; extend with Cisco DC Networking application patterns or scripted REST collectors for NDO endpoints.
• Index design: reserve `index=cisco_aci` (rename per governance) for APIC-facing lines plus an `index=cisco_ndo` (or merged) lane for cisco:mso:audit, **`cisco:ndo:audit`**, **`cisco:mso:health`**, **`cisco:ndo:health`**, **`cisco:mso:syslog`** so role limits stay auditable.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Package Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI (Splunkbase 4022) on forwarders/search heads that parse fabric syslog into **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, preserving multiSite fault text for **`multiSite::NotPresent`** correlation.
(2) Install or enable Cisco DC Networking application assets (Splunkbase 7777) when you want curated Cisco dashboards—still wire bespoke REST scripted inputs for **NDO**.
(3) Schedule HTTPS collectors against **NDO** **`GET https://<ndo-host>/mso/api/v1/sites`**, **`GET .../mso/api/v1/schemas`**, **`GET .../mso/api/v1/audit-records`**—OAuth or token schemes vary by release; normalize JSON into **`cisco:mso:audit`**/**`cisco:ndo:audit`** with **`audit_action`**, **`username`**, **`target_dn`**, **`deploy_outcome`**/**`outcome`**, **`schema_id`**, **`template_name`**, **`site_id`**, **`time_to_sync_seconds`** when exporters compute latency.
(4) Poll GET .../mso/api/v1 health facets that populate **`cisco:mso:health`**/**`cisco:ndo:health`** carrying **`site_state`**, **`deploy_phase`** strings spanning **Configuration In Progress** → **Deployed Successfully** → **Out-of-Sync** → **Apply Pending** → **Failed**, and ingest mso:syslog frames when forwarded from **NDO** nodes.
(5) Land **IPN**/**ISN** router syslog on **`cisco:nexus:syslog`** or operator-normalized **`syslog`** sourcetypes—highlight OSPF/BGP-IPv4 neighbor churn impacting reachability toward Multi-Site Loopback routes.
(6) Stream spine **`bgp:evpn:asbr`** telemetry—often modular telemetry from **`cisco:aci:bgp_evpn`** pivots renamed—capturing **`bgp_session_state`**, **`multisite_loopback_ip`**, **`site_id`** tie-ins for EVPN overlays spanning sites.
(7) Configure **Cloud APIC** **`GET https://<cloud-apic>/api/v1/audit`** (plus **`cisco:capic:audit`**, **`cisco:capic:health`**) so AWS/Azure stretches participate in schema acknowledgement timelines beside on-prem audits.

Step 2 — Splunk SPL search and alert configuration
Bookmark aci_uc1817_ndo_multisite_health as the operational scheduled search; throttle site_id + schema_id keys when paging.

```spl
index=cisco_aci (
 sourcetype IN ("cisco:mso:audit","cisco:ndo:audit","cisco:mso:health","cisco:ndo:health","cisco:mso:syslog","bgp:evpn:asbr")
 OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(multiSite::NotPresent|multi-site|intersite|schema.?deploy|mso:)"))
 ) earliest=-24h@m
| eval lane=case(sourcetype IN ("cisco:mso:audit","cisco:ndo:audit"),"audit",sourcetype IN ("cisco:mso:health","cisco:ndo:health"),"health",sourcetype=="cisco:mso:syslog","mso_syslog",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog","apic_ms",sourcetype=="bgp:evpn:asbr","bgp","other")
| eval site_id=trim(coalesce(site_id,siteId,site_uuid,site_code,managed_site_id,""))
| eval site_state=lower(trim(coalesce(site_state,siteState,siteOperationalState,"")))
| eval schema_id=trim(coalesce(schema_id,mso_schema_id,schemaId,""))
| eval template_name=trim(coalesce(template_name,templateName,policy_template,""))
| eval deploy_outcome=lower(trim(coalesce(deploy_outcome,outcome,audit_action_outcome,"")))
| eval audit_action_u=upper(trim(coalesce(audit_action,action,"")))
| eval deploy_phase_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(deployment_phase,deploy_phase,schema_phase,sync_phase,"")))
| eval time_to_sync_seconds=tonumber(coalesce(time_to_sync_seconds,time_to_sync_sec,sync_latency_seconds,""))
| eval multisite_loopback_ip=trim(coalesce(multisite_loopback_ip,multisite_loopback,loopback_ip,""))
| eval bgp_session_state=lower(trim(coalesce(bgp_session_state,bgp_state,session_state,neighbor_state,"")))
| eval isn_latency_hint_ms=tonumber(coalesce(isn_latency_ms,isn_rtt_ms,ipn_latency_ms,""))
| eval risk_audit=if(lane=="audit" AND (deploy_outcome IN ("failed","queued","partial-deploy") OR match(deploy_phase_lc,"(?i)(out.of.sync|failed|apply.pending)")),"1","0")
| eval risk_health=if(lane=="health" AND (match(site_state,"(?i)(critical.comm.failure|site.outage|reachability.degraded)") OR match(deploy_phase_lc,"(?i)out.of.sync")),"1","0")
| eval risk_fault=if(lane=="apic_ms" AND match(_raw,"(?i)multiSite::NotPresent"),"1","0")
| eval risk_bgp=if(lane=="bgp" AND NOT match(bgp_session_state,"(?i)established"),"1","0")
| eval slo_breach=if(isnotnull(time_to_sync_seconds) AND time_to_sync_seconds>300,"1","0")
| eval velocity_hot=if(isnotnull(time_to_sync_seconds) AND time_to_sync_seconds>360 AND deploy_phase_lc!="deployed successfully","1","0")
| where risk_audit="1" OR risk_health="1" OR risk_fault="1" OR risk_bgp="1" OR slo_breach="1" OR velocity_hot="1"
| bin _time span=5m as win5
| stats count as evcount max(time_to_sync_seconds) as time_to_sync_max latest(deploy_phase_lc) as deploy_phase_latest latest(template_name) as template_latest latest(site_state) as site_state_latest latest(bgp_session_state) as bgp_state_latest latest(multisite_loopback_ip) as loopback_latest latest(isn_latency_hint_ms) as isn_ms_latest values(deploy_outcome) as outcome_vals values(audit_action_u) as audit_action_vals max(risk_audit) as m_audit max(risk_health) as m_health max(risk_fault) as m_fault max(risk_bgp) as m_bgp max(slo_breach) as m_slo max(velocity_hot) as m_vel by win5 site_id schema_id
| eval breach_score=m_audit+m_health+m_fault+m_bgp
| eval pred_hot=if(time_to_sync_max>300 AND breach_score>=1,1,0)
| sort - time_to_sync_max - m_fault - pred_hot
| head 260
| table win5 site_id schema_id evcount time_to_sync_max deploy_phase_latest template_latest site_state_latest bgp_state_latest loopback_latest isn_ms_latest outcome_vals m_audit m_health m_fault m_bgp m_slo m_vel pred_hot breach_score
```

Interpretation notes: **`risk_audit`** focuses on stalled **Out-of-Sync** or **partial-deploy** outcomes, **`risk_health`** captures **Critical Comm Failure**/**Site Outage**/**Reachability Degraded** text, **`risk_fault`** isolates **`multiSite::NotPresent`**, **`risk_bgp`** marks **BGP-EVPN ASBR** sessions not **established**, and **`time_to_sync_seconds`** drives **SLO** math—tighten **`300`** seconds to **180** when your team standardizes faster windows. Raise alert severities when **`pred_hot`** scores **1** alongside **`breach_score`** ≥ **2**.

Step 3 — Validate
• In NDO UI Sites → select site → **Operational** tabs, compare **`site_state`** plus health score widgets to Splunk **`cisco:ndo:health`** fields for the same **`site_id`** within one poll interval.
• In NDO Schemas, open a schema and confirm **deploy** phase mirrors **`deploy_phase_latest`** extracted in Splunk; mismatches often mean duplicated collectors racing the same **`schema_id`**.
• In APIC GUI multi-site sections, validate **intersite** banners against Splunk timelines—use this to separate **APIC** fabric issues from pure **NDO** queue backlog.
• In Cloud APIC consoles for AWS/Azure regions, confirm controller availability during tests; **`cisco:capic:health`** gaps with still-healthy on-prem **`cisco:mso:audit`** lines often mean cloud-side delay not on-prem breakage.
• For BGP validation, SSH to spine **BGP-EVPN ASBR** peers and compare neighbor state strings to Splunk **`bgp_session_state`**.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard layout (NDO Multi-Site Executive workbook suggestion):
• Row 1 — Matrix tile per site_id showing site_state_latest hues (Up, Reachable, Provisioning, Critical Comm Failure, Site Outage, Reachability Degraded).
• Row 2 — Time-series time_to_sync_seconds per **`schema_id`** with Deployed Successfully markers versus Out-of-Sync overlays—surface pred_hot spikes.
• Row 3 — Heat-map isn_ms_latest vs **`site_id`** when WAN probes feed isn_latency_hint_ms fields (derive indirectly via time_to_sync_seconds derivatives when probes absent).
• Row 4 — **BGP-EVPN ASBR** grid **`multisite_loopback_ip`** × **`site_id`** with bgp_session_state badges.
• Row 5 — Forecast ribbon estimating **`time_to_sync_seconds`** velocity—highlight sustained elevation beyond baseline multiples implying IPN jitter.

Routing: route flag_fault positives with multiSite::NotPresent to backbone engineers; schema stuck Out-of-Sync pairs go policy automation; risk_bgp rows bridge WAN + spine routing guilds.

Step 5 — Tuning and extensions
• Correlate with **UC-18.1.7** APIC cluster backlog signals—controller saturation lengthens **`time_to_sync_seconds`** without **WAN** faults.
• Blend **UC-18.1.5** tenant audit lanes by joining schema_id to `tn-{name}` hints in `target_dn_lc` extracts for change forensics—not the same blast-radius story, but complementary.
• Maintain suppression macros keyed to ndo_cluster_upgrade, `isn_wan_maintenance_window`, `cloud_apic_quarterly_upgrade`, `ipn_router_firmware_reload`, `site_onboarding_window`, `cab_mass_schema_rollout`, `dr_failover_exercise_isolation` tags from your change calendar.
• Integrate **UC-18.3.14** underlay topology integrity when upland IPN routes oscillate yet NDO remains green.
• Enrich **Cloud APIC** AWS/Azure native telemetry streams (flow logs, LB health) only for triage context—keep Splunk searches index-pure unless governance allows cross-index joins.

Failure focus: duplicated **REST** collectors double-count evcount until you add `collector_id` dedup keys. HTTP 401 storms on `/mso/api/v1` endpoints mean token refresh misses—inspect Heavy Forwarder splunkd.log. **`bgp_session_state`** nulls often mean telemetry not yet extracted—add FIELDALIAS stanzas when vendor JSON uses camelCase variants. Sparse **`site_id`** fields suggest default **KV** extraction—promote explicit props for cisco:mso:audit.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci (
 sourcetype IN ("cisco:mso:audit","cisco:ndo:audit","cisco:mso:health","cisco:ndo:health","cisco:mso:syslog","bgp:evpn:asbr")
 OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(multiSite::NotPresent|multi-site|intersite|schema.?deploy|mso:)"))
 ) earliest=-24h@m
| eval lane=case(sourcetype IN ("cisco:mso:audit","cisco:ndo:audit"),"audit",sourcetype IN ("cisco:mso:health","cisco:ndo:health"),"health",sourcetype=="cisco:mso:syslog","mso_syslog",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog","apic_ms",sourcetype=="bgp:evpn:asbr","bgp","other")
| eval site_id=trim(coalesce(site_id,siteId,site_uuid,site_code,managed_site_id,""))
| eval site_state=lower(trim(coalesce(site_state,siteState,siteOperationalState,"")))
| eval schema_id=trim(coalesce(schema_id,mso_schema_id,schemaId,""))
| eval template_name=trim(coalesce(template_name,templateName,policy_template,""))
| eval deploy_outcome=lower(trim(coalesce(deploy_outcome,outcome,audit_action_outcome,"")))
| eval audit_action_u=upper(trim(coalesce(audit_action,action,"")))
| eval deploy_phase_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(deployment_phase,deploy_phase,schema_phase,sync_phase,"")))
| eval time_to_sync_seconds=tonumber(coalesce(time_to_sync_seconds,time_to_sync_sec,sync_latency_seconds,""))
| eval multisite_loopback_ip=trim(coalesce(multisite_loopback_ip,multisite_loopback,loopback_ip,""))
| eval bgp_session_state=lower(trim(coalesce(bgp_session_state,bgp_state,session_state,neighbor_state,"")))
| eval isn_latency_hint_ms=tonumber(coalesce(isn_latency_ms,isn_rtt_ms,ipn_latency_ms,""))
| eval risk_audit=if(lane=="audit" AND (deploy_outcome IN ("failed","queued","partial-deploy") OR match(deploy_phase_lc,"(?i)(out.of.sync|failed|apply.pending)")),"1","0")
| eval risk_health=if(lane=="health" AND (match(site_state,"(?i)(critical.comm.failure|site.outage|reachability.degraded)") OR match(deploy_phase_lc,"(?i)out.of.sync")),"1","0")
| eval risk_fault=if(lane=="apic_ms" AND match(_raw,"(?i)multiSite::NotPresent"),"1","0")
| eval risk_bgp=if(lane=="bgp" AND NOT match(bgp_session_state,"(?i)established"),"1","0")
| eval slo_breach=if(isnotnull(time_to_sync_seconds) AND time_to_sync_seconds>300,"1","0")
| eval velocity_hot=if(isnotnull(time_to_sync_seconds) AND time_to_sync_seconds>360 AND deploy_phase_lc!="deployed successfully","1","0")
| where risk_audit="1" OR risk_health="1" OR risk_fault="1" OR risk_bgp="1" OR slo_breach="1" OR velocity_hot="1"
| bin _time span=5m as win5
| stats count as evcount max(time_to_sync_seconds) as time_to_sync_max latest(deploy_phase_lc) as deploy_phase_latest latest(template_name) as template_latest latest(site_state) as site_state_latest latest(bgp_session_state) as bgp_state_latest latest(multisite_loopback_ip) as loopback_latest latest(isn_latency_hint_ms) as isn_ms_latest values(deploy_outcome) as outcome_vals values(audit_action_u) as audit_action_vals max(risk_audit) as m_audit max(risk_health) as m_health max(risk_fault) as m_fault max(risk_bgp) as m_bgp max(slo_breach) as m_slo max(velocity_hot) as m_vel by win5 site_id schema_id
| eval breach_score=m_audit+m_health+m_fault+m_bgp
| eval pred_hot=if(time_to_sync_max>300 AND breach_score>=1,1,0)
| sort - time_to_sync_max - m_fault - pred_hot
| head 260
| table win5 site_id schema_id evcount time_to_sync_max deploy_phase_latest template_latest site_state_latest bgp_state_latest loopback_latest isn_ms_latest outcome_vals m_audit m_health m_fault m_bgp m_slo m_vel pred_hot breach_score
```

## Visualization

(1) **site_id** × **`site_state`** health matrix. (2) **`time_to_sync_seconds`** trend per **`schema_id`**. (3) **ISN**/**IPN** latency heat when hints exist. (4) **BGP-EVPN ASBR** session grid on **`multisite_loopback_ip`**. (5) **`pred_hot`** ribbon for predictive schema velocity.
